profile_positioning.title: Founder of Ammplug and systems engineer
profile_positioning.summary: Founder of Ammplug and a systems engineer who not only builds his own micro-SaaS product, but also helps others create smarter AI systems for business.

product.name: Ammplug
product.description: A plugin library for B2B and B2C users that helps no-code experts, non-technical designers, marketers, digital agencies, and end users solve custom cases without writing code or relying heavily on automation platforms.
core_value_points: Install plugin-based capabilities without writing custom code
Cover rare API integration cases that cannot be connected directly
Help digital agencies speed up implementation with a broader plugin library
Include local AI agents inside some plugins to help users work with them

priority_audiences: No-code experts working with Webflow, Shopify, Zapier, Make, and n8n
Designers and marketers working with Webflow
Digital agencies that want to reduce development cost
Side-project builders who want to create products independently and save money
primary_goal: Create interest in Ammplug and its plugins by building trust through AI topics and then converting that attention into product curiosity.

business_stage.status_note: The plugin library is still in development and not yet positioned as broadly available.
content_implications: Focus on warming up the audience and building interest
Favor curiosity, resonance, and engagement over direct conversion asks
Avoid CTAs that imply a mature, ready-to-roll product rollout

near_term_promotion_goals: Build curiosity around Ammplug and its plugin direction
Make people like, comment on, and remember the posts
Warm up experts, agencies, and founders before stronger commercial asks
Communicate the future value of the plugin ecosystem without overselling availability
engagement_objectives: Encourage comments from founders and operators

strengthen_topics: AI in the context of Ammplug plugin architecture
Practical business value of plugin systems
content_focus.avoid_topics_note: No explicit avoid-topics were provided. One answer appeared to repeat desired architecture themes, so this field is intentionally left empty until clarified.

style_rules: Content should be understandable for non-technical readers
Posts should create desire to try the product
Posts should communicate sharp practical value
Avoid technical overload when it is not necessary
Avoid being too abstract when the post idea can be made concrete
Translate architecture ideas into simple business-facing language
Use light irony or dry humor when it makes the post feel more alive
Prefer witty contrasts and recognizable real-world metaphors over flat explanation
Do not turn the tone into meme content or clownish sarcasm
Use short standalone lines and short paragraphs instead of long blocks
Use contrast, rhythm, and line breaks to make the post easy to scan quickly

number_policy.rule: Numbers are allowed when they help explain an idea, but if they are not confirmed facts they must be framed clearly as example, hypothetical, illustrative, or directional rather than claimed as real measured results.

disallowed: Direct product availability CTA that implies launch readiness
